channel.title: Reuben Ingber
channel.description: Articles on fatherhood, engineering management, ADHD, books, and life by Reuben Ingber.

item.title: Something New I've Been Building
item.description: Introducing Photo Sessions with Reuben Ingber — why I'm doing this, why I'm practicing on my own kids every day, and why I'm starting with free test shoots this September.

item.title: I Added a Few New Pages to This Site
item.description: Four small additions to reubeningber.com — a /friends page, a /now page, a /uses page, and a colophon — inspired by the old-school personal web and built with a chatbot doing most of the typing.

item.title: Father's Day Without My Dad
item.description: My dad died the same year I became a father. Every Father's Day since, I get to be celebrated while quietly missing the person I most want to celebrate.

item.title: Knicks Shirt Maker: A One-Day Build
item.description: Taylor Swift wore a "STEVIE KNICKS" shirt to Game 4 of the NBA Finals and I built a free browser tool to let anyone make their own version — same arched orange lettering, instant PNG download, shipped same day.

item.title: Favorite Things - May 2026
item.description: A month of small joys and big moments, from Coney Island and Legoland to photography, good food, art, and time with family.

item.title: I'm the emergency contact. The school still calls my wife.
item.description: I'm the default parent in our house. Not because I'm more nurturing — because I work from home. Here's what that arrangement actually looks like, what it costs both of us, and why the infrastructure still hasn't caught up.

item.title: Stop Letting Summer Happen to You
item.description: Every year summer either happens to you or you make it happen. Here's how my wife and I plan our entire summer in one night — camps, trips, date nights, and the four-week gap that NYC families are left to figure out on their own.

item.title: AI Is Making It Hard to Step Away
item.description: I'm not afraid AI will take my job. I'm afraid someone using AI better than me will be seen as more effective. That's a different fear — and a harder one to reason with.

item.title: We built a bookcase
item.description: A Saturday father-son project where we designed and built a custom bookcase sized for Zeke's favorite books.

item.title: Three Books to Read When Your New Year's Resolutions Have Already Fallen Apart
item.description: Your New Year's resolutions have already fallen apart. Here are three books that actually help: Atomic Habits, Meditations for Mortals, and So Good They Can't Ignore You - practical frameworks for building systems that work with human nature, not against it.

item.title: The Book That Changed How I Think About My Kids and Screens
item.description: Why every dad should read Jonathan Haidt's The Anxious Generation - a wake-up call about smartphones, childhood development, and practical ways to protect our kids' mental health while building their real-world confidence.

item.title: 2026: The Year of Doing the Work (and Taking the Kids With Me)
item.description: Instead of rigid resolutions, I'm building a container for 2026—one Misogi, six mini adventures, four habits, and 26 intentions that paint a picture of the year I actually want to live.

item.title: Choosing Two Out of Three: My 2025 Reality Check
item.description: A year of shifting priorities - choosing family and work over personal goals, and finding perspective in the unexpected changes life brings.

item.title: The Very Best Books I Read in 2025
item.description: A reflection on the five books that stayed with me most in 2025 — from survival stories to productivity wisdom — and why reading 51 books felt more meaningful than chasing 100.

item.title: How My ADHD Diagnosis Changed the Way I Show Up as a Dad
item.description: Getting diagnosed with ADHD as a parent changed how I show up for my wife and kids — quieting the noise, finding routines, and learning to give more grace.

item.title: The Joy of Obsession: Why Going All In Matters
item.description: A reflection on the magic of going all in — from childhood obsessions to adult hobbies — and how caring deeply can make us happier, healthier, and more alive.

item.title: What Getting Diagnosed with ADHD at 30 Taught Me About Work
item.description: Getting diagnosed with ADHD at 30 changed how I work, focus, and forgive myself. Here's what I learned about attention, productivity, and self-compassion.

item.title: Brick by Brick: Holding Onto What Matters
item.description: A reflection on rediscovering Lego with my son, the quiet joy of screen-free play, and the bittersweet moment when our kids start to build worlds of their own.

item.title: Better Than Before, and the Season of Starting Over
item.description: Gretchen Rubin's Better Than Before reminded me that habit-building after kids isn't about trying harder — it's about finding small, forgiving loops that fit your life now.

item.title: October Favorite Things
item.description: October 2025 highlights: celebrating a 10-year anniversary in Paris, officiating my brother's wedding, fall family moments, and building a travel site with AI.

item.title: Why Every Dad Should Have a Digital Camera
item.description: A dedicated digital camera keeps you present with your kids, off your phone, and produces wall-worthy family photos without an expensive professional photoshoot.

item.title: The Solutions Log: A Manager’s System to Capture Impact (So My Reviews Are Easy)
item.description: Stop struggling with self-reviews. The Solutions Log is a lightweight Markdown system for engineering managers to capture impact in real time and nail every review.

item.title: Building a Paris Travel Site with AI in One Night
item.description: How I built a custom Paris travel website with GitHub Copilot in four hours — self-contained HTML pages, no build tools, 67 restaurant picks, and offline support.

item.title: The one parenting hack I have not seen on Instagram
item.description: Use your kid's tablet passcode to teach them your cell phone number — a clever screen-time trick that builds a real safety skill without any extra effort.

item.title: Why Every Dad Should Read The Anxious Generation
item.description: Jonathan Haidt's The Anxious Generation explains how smartphones reshaped childhood — and why dads need to read it before handing their kid a phone.

item.title: 5 Things to Do Every Day to Be a Happier Dad
item.description: Five small daily habits that make you a more present, patient father — from waking up early to giving kids your undivided attention for just 10 minutes.

item.title: 35 Things I’ve Learned In 35 Years
item.description: 35 hard-won life lessons on turning 35 — from reading every day and prioritizing sleep, to love, parenting, money, and why systems beat goals every time.

item.title: How One Index Card Can Drastically Improve Your Journaling?
item.description: A single index card with 8 journaling prompts ends the blank-page problem for good — the simple system that's kept a daily journaling practice alive for 3+ years.

item.title: Take Your Reading to a New Level and Reread a Book This Year
item.description: Rereading books unlocks new insights as you grow — here's why reading the same book in a different format (audio, digital, print) is the smartest reading upgrade.

item.title: The 3 Best Things I Purchased in 2022
item.description: My three most worthwhile purchases in 2022: a grill pan that changed weeknight dinners, a bigger desk with a light bar, and Lego sets as a screen-free hobby.

item.title: Annual Themes
item.description: Ditch rigid New Year's resolutions for an annual theme — a guiding word or phrase that keeps you focused on consistency over perfection throughout the year.

item.title: It costs nothing to be nice
item.description: A Christmas Eve Cracker Barrel story about giving grace to a struggling waitress on her first day — and why small kindnesses cost nothing but mean everything.
